relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The leaders of Gilead bring a new concern created by the daughters' inheritance victory.
They acknowledge that Zelophehad's daughters rightly received land.
Their fear is that marriage could move that land into another tribe.
Even Jubilee, they argue, would not restore it to Manasseh.
Moses says the tribal concern is valid within the inheritance system.
The daughters retain a measure of choice, but only within their father's clan.
The ruling protects tribal land from crossing boundaries.
Every inheriting daughter is placed under the same endogamous rule.
The inheritance map is guarded from movement between tribes.
The daughters act within the command.
Mahlah, Tirzah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Noah marry within extended family.
Numbers closes in the plains of Moab with these final commands.

contemplative_note: Numbers ends where law and patriarchy remain unresolved. The daughters changed Torah by speaking, yet their freedom is narrowed to protect tribal property. Their courage is the stronger inheritance: excluded people can name what law has failed to see, and law can be made to answer.
